summary: Import-time fetch and eval of attacker-controlled GitHub Gist
details: On require/import, index.js fetches the GitHub Gist at https://gist.github.com/metabrainai000/198a0bbec7a6018e9250615d26e37b90.js, extracts the first file's content, and passes it directly to eval(). The Gist is mutable and controlled by an external GitHub user unrelated to the @sqlite-node namespace, so any project that imports this package executes whatever JavaScript that Gist returns at the time of import, with full Node privileges. Package metadata reinforces the malicious shape: a placeholder description, a repository URL pointing to an unrelated project (array-utl_nodelump), and a name that squats on the @sqlite-node scope while providing no SQLite functionality. There is no integrity pin, no hash check, and no documented purpose for the remote fetch.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
